_spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: Loop 3's parse extraction (now visible) lifts architecture, simplicity, domain modeling, test strategy and credibility. This loop clears the dead wrapper. The remaining gap to top-tier is that search_boards still fuses filtering, the network date-scan and all terminal rendering in ~690 lines, with two duplicated render blocks.

architecture_quality.proof: per-board parse now a separate tested Module (_parse_board_html, commit 0d964bb); search_boards no longer fuses parsing. Residual: it still fuses filtering, date-scan and rendering (soundboard-snag.py:702-1396)

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; immutable instance attrs

domain_modeling.proof: ParsedBoard NamedTuple added (commit 0d964bb) — a second named domain record. Residual: sounds_info still bare 2-tuples, approx_source stringly-typed

data_flow.proof: named-field contracts; residual: main re-filters results (soundboard-snag.py:~1585) duplicating search_boards' filter

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: search_boards shrank ~734->~694 after the parse extraction (commit 0d964bb). Residual: dead wrapper (fixed this loop) + duplicated render blocks (1184 vs 1361; 1274 vs 1386)

test_strategy.proof: ParseBoardHtmlTests added (commit 0d964bb) — the per-board parse Interface is now fixture-tested; 28 tests total. Residual: search_boards' filtering/date-scan/rendering paths untested

credibility.proof: parse logic now backed by direct tests (commit 0d964bb); two named domain records; code remains honest with no fake-clean

strengths: Two extraction loops (BoardResult, _parse_board_html) have each been behavior-preserving and independently reviewed — the refactor history is honest
The pure helpers and the per-board parse are both fixture-tested at their real Interfaces
Synchronous design + defensive sanitization remain real strengths

findings.title: Dead pass-through wrapper _fetch_last_modified
findings.why_it_matters: Unused indirection adds reading cost and a second name for one behavior with no caller to justify it.
findings.what_is_wrong: _fetch_last_modified wrapped _fetch_last_modified_detailed and discarded the diagnostic, but nothing in the module (or the standalone debug_track_dates.py) called it.
evidence: grep confirms zero callers in soundboard-snag.py and debug_track_dates.py
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader had to confirm it was unused before trusting the live path.
findings.why_weakens_submission: A pass-through wrapper with no caller is dead weight the deletion test removes cleanly.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Delete _fetch_last_modified. Zero-risk subtractive win taken first (Meta-Rule 5) before the larger render/filter extraction.

findings.title: search_boards still fuses filtering, the network date-scan and all terminal rendering (~690 lines)
findings.why_it_matters: The remaining biggest structural deduction: filter evaluation and result rendering are pure/near-pure but cannot be tested because they are inlined in the network loop.
findings.what_is_wrong: After the parse extraction, search_boards still inlines: the filter evaluation (meets_filters / filter_reasons), the best-effort date-scan reduction, and two large rendering sections (per-board summary + final results render).
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers still hold ~690 lines in one scope to change filtering or output.
findings.locality_impact: A filter or formatting change still risks the rest of the function via shared locals.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Filtering and formatting are testable logic kept unreachable by fusion with I/O; this caps architecture, simplicity and test strategy.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Over the next loops, extract a pure filter evaluator (parsed + thresholds -> pass/fail + reasons) and pure render/format helpers (BoardResult -> str), each fixture-tested; do not extract the network date-scan loop itself (it is I/O-bound). No class hierarchy.

findings.title: Date-display and skipped-breakdown rendering duplicated near-verbatim
findings.why_it_matters: Two copies of the same presentation logic must be kept in sync by hand; they will drift.
findings.what_is_wrong: The approx-updated date block and the skipped-buckets breakdown block each appear twice with near-identical bodies.
findings.leverage_impact: A formatting change must be made in two places.
findings.locality_impact: Drift between the two copies produces inconsistent output for the same data.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Duplicate presentation logic is a simplification deduction; it is subsumed by the render extraction in F-006.
findings.minimal_correction_path: When extracting render helpers for F-006, fold the two duplicated blocks into single module-level format helpers taking a BoardResult and the date-stats map.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: F-004 passes the Deletion test: removing _fetch_last_modified makes complexity vanish (zero callers), not reappear.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: Removing dead code first keeps the subsequent render/filter extraction (F-006) cleaner.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not, while deleting the wrapper, also touch _fetch_last_modified_detailed or the cache — they are the live path.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: No test change needed — the wrapper had no callers and no tests; the 28-test suite stays green as a regression guard.

backlog.title: Delete dead wrapper _fetch_last_modified (F-004)
backlog.why_it_matters: Zero-risk subtractive win cleared before the larger render/filter extraction.

backlog.title: Extract pure filter evaluation and render/format helpers from search_boards (F-006)
backlog.why_it_matters: Largest remaining structural lever; makes filtering and formatting testable and shrinks the function.

backlog.title: Fold the duplicated date-display and skipped-breakdown render blocks (F-005)
backlog.why_it_matters: Removes drift-prone copies; folded into the F-006 render extraction.

deepening_candidates.candidate_module: Filter evaluation + result rendering inside search_boards
deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F-006 — filtering and formatting are pure but inlined in the network loop, so untestable
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: There is no filter or render Interface; both are inlined across ~690 lines of one function.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: Pure filter evaluation (parsed fields + thresholds -> pass/fail + reasons) and pure BoardResult-to-string render helpers.
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py asserts filter pass/fail and rendered strings without network.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Extract a pure _evaluate_filters(...) returning (meets, reasons); call it from the loop.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not extract the network date-scan loop; do not add a renderer class hierarchy.

builder_notes.pattern: Clear the certain subtractive win before the big refactor
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A zero-risk deletion (dead code) sits alongside a larger structural change in the backlog.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Take the deletion first in its own commit; it shrinks the surface the larger refactor must reason about.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: delete an uncalled wrapper before extracting the render helpers from the same file.

builder_notes.pattern: Deletion test before removing any wrapper
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A thin function delegates to another; you suspect it is unused.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: grep every caller across the whole repo (including standalone scripts) before deleting; confirm complexity vanishes, not relocates.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: confirm both soundboard-snag.py and debug_track_dates.py have zero references.

builder_notes.pattern: Name the next structural target precisely once the headline one is fixed
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: After resolving the biggest finding, a vaguer 'still large' feeling remains.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Re-derive the residual into a concrete finding with file:line evidence (which sub-blocks are still fused) so the backlog stays honest.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: 'search_boards still inlines filter eval (1095-1150) and render (1332-1390)' is actionable; 'search_boards is big' is not.

narrative: Place — a good app, climbing steadily. Loop 3's parse extraction now shows up across five dimensions, and this loop takes the certain subtractive win (dead wrapper). The honest residual is named precisely: search_boards still fuses filter evaluation and two render sections in ~690 lines, with duplicated render blocks. Ownership and concurrency remain trustworthy. Next loops should extract a pure filter evaluator and render helpers (testable, subtractive in cognitive load) while resisting any renderer class hierarchy the single-file design does not need.

loop_result.what_changed: Deleted the dead pass-through wrapper _fetch_last_modified (4 lines + surrounding blank line) from soundboard-snag.py; the live path uses _fetch_last_modified_detailed and the fetch_last_modified_cached closure.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 28 tests, all OK; --help exits 0; grep confirms _fetch_last_modified (non-detailed) no longer appears in soundboard-snag.py and debug_track_dates.py never referenced it; net -6 lines.

implementation_review.reason: The dead 4-line wrapper _fetch_last_modified (plus surrounding blank lines) was deleted; zero remaining references to the non-detailed name exist in soundboard-snag.py or debug_track_dates.py; _fetch_last_modified_detailed and the fetch_last_modified_cached closure are untouched; the diff is purely subtractive with no costume layer, suppression, or regression.
